body:
Hi Allen,

Great work pulling the zoning overlay maps together for the two case cities. Before our Zoom on Monday, can you post your weekly research notes to the Confluence wiki? Just the usual: what you covered, any data gaps, and the one question you're stuck on.

We can use the call to unblock the displacement-rate comparison.

Best,
Priya
snippet: Great work pulling the zoning overlay maps together for the two case cities. Bef...
subject: Weekly research notes before Monday

body:
Hi Priya,

Just posted this week's notes to Confluence. Short version: both case cities are cleaned and the zoning overlays are done. The data gap is the 2015-2019 rental vacancy series for the smaller city, the open dataset has two missing years I'm trying to backfill.

My stuck question is whether to normalize displacement rates by tract or by block group, they tell slightly different stories. Saved it for Monday.

Thanks,
Allen
snippet: Just posted this week's notes to Confluence. Short version: both case cities are...
subject: Re: Weekly research notes before Monday

body:
Allen,

Hope the Whitfield summer is going well. I wanted to check in on the thesis proposal before the term picks up. The proposal is due October 5, and I'd like to see the case-city scoping settled well before then.

If it's useful, grab a slot on my Calendly for office hours, I have openings most afternoons this month.

Prof. Marsh
snippet: Hope the Whitfield summer is going well. I wanted to check in on the thesis prop...
subject: Thesis proposal - summer check-in

body:
Hi Prof. Marsh,

Thank you, the summer placement has been a great fit for the thesis. I've scoped the proposal to two New England case cities and the displacement-and-zoning angle from my junior paper carries over cleanly.

I'll book an office-hours slot this week so I can walk you through the case selection. I'd value your read on the methodology before I lock it.

Best,
Allen
snippet: Thank you, the summer placement has been a great fit for the thesis. I've scoped...
subject: Re: Thesis proposal - summer check-in

body:
Good. Send me the draft proposal a week before the October 5 deadline so I have time to give real feedback, not a rushed read. Two pages is plenty at this stage, the question and the case logic are what matter.

See you at office hours.

Prof. Marsh
snippet: Good. Send me the draft proposal a week before the October 5 deadline so I have ...
subject: Re: Thesis proposal - summer check-in

body:
Hi Dr. Whitmore,

I'm applying to MPP programs this cycle, with Harvard Kennedy and Georgetown McCourt as early-action (deadline November 14). Would you be willing to write me a recommendation letter? You supervised my junior research paper on zoning and displacement, which is the throughline for all my applications.

Happy to send my CV, statement-of-purpose draft, and the deadline list whenever works.

Thank you,
Allen Weber
snippet: I'm applying to MPP programs this cycle, with Harvard Kennedy and Georgetown McC...
subject: Recommendation letter request - MPP applications

body:
Allen,

Glad to. Your junior paper was one of the stronger ones in the seminar, so I can write a substantive letter. Send me the CV, the SOP draft, and a spreadsheet with each program, portal, and deadline. A short reminder a week before each due date helps too.

Which programs beyond the two early-action ones should I expect?

Dr. Whitmore
snippet: Glad to. Your junior paper was one of the stronger ones in the seminar, so I can...
subject: Re: Recommendation letter request - MPP applications

body:
Thank you, that means a lot.

The full list is HKS and Georgetown McCourt (early action, Nov 14), then Tufts Fletcher and Johns Hopkins SAIS (regular, Feb 1). I'll share the tracker with portals and deadlines today, and I'm using the expanded junior paper as my writing sample.

I'll send a reminder a week ahead of each one.

Allen
snippet: Thank you, that means a lot. The full list is HKS and Georgetown McCourt (early ...
subject: Re: Recommendation letter request - MPP applications

body:
Got the tracker, thanks, this is exactly what I needed. I'll plan to have the early-action letters submitted by November 10 so there's a buffer before the 14th. Keep me posted if any program adds a supplemental form.

Good luck with the rest of the packet.

Dr. Whitmore
snippet: Got the tracker, thanks, this is exactly what I needed. I'll plan to have the ea...
subject: Re: Recommendation letter request - MPP applications

body: Yo Allen, starting to map out my Boston trip. Looking at the weekend of October 17, I can take the train up Saturday morning. Thinking Pho Basil for dinner like old times, then drinks around Harvard Square? Let me know if that weekend's clear before I lock the tickets.
snippet: Yo Allen, starting to map out my Boston trip. Looking at the weekend of October ...
subject: Boston visit - October 17?

body:
Oct 17 is clear, lock it in. I'll get us a Pho Basil reservation for 7. The #9 and a Thai iced tea are calling.

You can crash on our couch in Allston so you're not paying for a place. Just bring the policy gossip from DC.

- Allen
snippet: Oct 17 is clear, lock it in. I'll get us a Pho Basil reservation for 7. The #9 a...
subject: Re: Boston visit - October 17?

body:
Couch works, appreciate it. Train gets into South Station around 1pm Saturday, so I'll have time to drop my bag before dinner. I've got a ton to tell you about the think-tank job, half of it is exactly the small-city policy stuff you're researching.

Want me to grab tickets for anything Saturday night or keep it low-key?
snippet: Couch works, appreciate it. Train gets into South Station around 1pm Saturday, s...
subject: Re: Boston visit - October 17?

body:
Keep it low-key, dinner and Harvard Square is plenty, I'll have a thesis seminar reading to dent on Sunday anyway. And yes, I want all the small-city policy intel, that's basically free thesis material.

Text me when you book the train and I'll put it on the calendar.

- Allen
snippet: Keep it low-key, dinner and Harvard Square is plenty, I'll have a thesis seminar...
subject: Re: Boston visit - October 17?

body: Hey Allen, Marcus wants to get the next Morningside customer newsletter out before the April 4 weekend. Could you take a first pass at the draft in Klaviyo? Same vibe as last time, the new cold brew and the summer hours. I'll review and Marcus sends it. No rush, just before Thursday if you can.
snippet: Hey Allen, Marcus wants to get the next Morningside customer newsletter out befo...
subject: Morningside newsletter draft

body:
On it. I'll draft it in Klaviyo this weekend, cold brew up top and the summer hours block at the bottom, and leave it in review for you before anything goes out. Won't hit send, that's all you and Marcus.

See you Saturday opener.

- Allen
snippet: On it. I'll draft it in Klaviyo this weekend, cold brew up top and the summer ho...
subject: Re: Morningside newsletter draft

body:
Hi Allen,

Mid-program check-in for the Whitfield cohort. Your summer stipend has been disbursed, so no action needed there. Two reminders: please keep posting your weekly notes to the cohort Confluence space, and drop a short update in the Slack channel so the other fellows can see what everyone's working on.

The mentor mid-point review with Priya is the week of April 6. Let me know if you have any blockers.

Best,
Harper Quinn
Whitfield Research Fellowship
snippet: Mid-program check-in for the Whitfield cohort. Your summer stipend has been disb...
subject: Whitfield summer - mid-program check-in

body:
Hi Harper,

Thanks for the check-in. Confirmed on the stipend, all set. I'm caught up on the Confluence notes and I'll post a project update to the cohort Slack today, the zoning-reform work at the New England Cities Initiative is on track.

The week of April 6 works for the mid-point review with Priya. No blockers right now.

Best,
Allen
snippet: Thanks for the check-in. Confirmed on the stipend, all set. I'm caught up on the...
subject: Re: Whitfield summer - mid-program check-in

body:
Perfect, thanks Allen. One more thing: you've been added to the Whitfield alumni newsletter (via ActiveCampaign) so you'll start seeing post-grad and career pieces, save anything useful for later. Details on the end-of-summer fellow showcase will go out in late April.

Keep up the strong work.

Harper
snippet: Perfect, thanks Allen. One more thing: you've been added to the Whitfield alumni...
subject: Re: Whitfield summer - mid-program check-in

body:
Hi Allen,

Harper flagged that our mid-point review lands the week of April 6. I have openings Monday April 6 at 2pm or Wednesday April 8 at 10am, both on Zoom. Pick whichever works and I'll send the invite.

Come with the displacement-rate comparison and the two-city normalization question from Monday's call, that's what I'd like to dig into.

Best,
Priya
snippet: Harper flagged that our mid-point review lands the week of April 6. I have opening...
subject: Mid-point review - scheduling the week of April 6

body:
Hi Priya,

Monday April 6 at 2pm works, let's lock that. I'll bring the updated displacement-rate comparison and come ready on the tract-vs-block-group normalization question.

I'll also have the backfilled 2015-2019 vacancy years in by then, so the smaller city's series will be complete.

Thanks,
Allen
snippet: Monday April 6 at 2pm works, let's lock that. I'll bring the updated displacement-...
subject: Re: Mid-point review - scheduling the week of April 6

body:
Allen,

Good progress. The versions I signed off on are the ones you sent after our last office hour, the Kennedy cut that opens on the zoning-and-displacement thread and the McCourt cut that leads with the data-analytics angle. The earlier all-purpose draft still floating in your folder reads thinner on the first-generation section, so stop pulling from it. Keep the first-generation paragraph identical across all four so your own story does not wander.

Prof. Marsh
snippet: Allen,  Good progress. The versions I signed off on are the ones you sent after our last o...
subject: Re: SOP notes -- use the latest tailored cuts

body:
Allen,

I have the Kennedy portal request in and submitted that one. I do not see the Georgetown request in my queue yet, so nothing is uploaded there on my end. If Georgetown is early action you will want to resend the invite so I can get it in before the close. For the two February programs there is time.

JW
snippet: Allen,  I have the Kennedy portal request in and submitted that one. I do not see the Geor...

Allen,

Confirming I uploaded my letters for both Kennedy and McCourt through their portals this week. The Fletcher and SAIS ones I will do closer to February. Let me know if any portal shows mine as missing so I can re-send.

Prof. Marsh
